note: Hand-authored consumer copy per trait (our own words), merged into the curated catalog. Keyed by trait_id. consumer_value = how to read this genetic tendency alongside your measured biomarkers and wearable data.

resting_heart_rate.consumer_value: Resting heart rate is meaningfully heritable but very trainable. Compare this inherited tendency with your wearable's resting-HR trend; a lower, stable resting rate usually reflects good cardiovascular fitness.

systolic_blood_pressure_levels.consumer_value: An inherited tendency toward higher or lower systolic (top-number) blood pressure. Repeated validated home or clinic readings are decisive; treat this as one input, not a verdict.

diastolic_blood_pressure_levels.consumer_value: An inherited tendency for diastolic (bottom-number) blood pressure. Confirm with repeated measured readings; lifestyle and the actual numbers take precedence.

ldl_cholesterol_levels.consumer_value: A tendency toward higher or lower LDL ('bad') cholesterol, a core driver of cardiovascular risk. A higher tendency is a reason to measure LDL or ApoB directly and act on the number.

hdl_cholesterol_levels.consumer_value: A tendency for HDL ('good') cholesterol. Use a current lipid panel to see your real level; the inherited signal is background context.

apolipoprotein_b_levels.consumer_value: ApoB counts the artery-clogging cholesterol particles that best predict heart-disease risk. A higher genetic tendency is a strong nudge to measure ApoB directly and keep it low.

apolipoprotein_a1_levels.consumer_value: ApoA1 is the main protein in HDL cholesterol. Read this tendency next to a measured ApoA1 or HDL result; the blood test is what counts.

qt_intervals.consumer_value: Genetic context for the heart's electrical recovery time (the QT interval). Most useful before starting medicines that can lengthen QT; pair with an ECG rather than acting on genetics alone.

blood_coagulation_factor_v_leiden_and_20.consumer_value: These clotting-factor variants raise the tendency to form blood clots. Worth flagging before long flights, surgery, or hormonal therapy, and if you have a personal or family history of clots; discuss with a clinician.

blood_glucose.consumer_value: An inherited tendency for fasting glucose. Read it beside your measured glucose, HbA1c, and any continuous-glucose data; the numbers, plus post-meal movement and sleep, are what you act on.

prediction_of_visceral_adipose_tissue.consumer_value: A tendency toward visceral fat (stored around the organs), which carries more cardiometabolic risk than fat elsewhere. Pair with a waist measurement; it responds well to sleep, resistance training, protein/fibre, and fewer refined carbs.

body_fat_percentage.consumer_value: An inherited tendency for body-fat level. Compare with a measured method (DEXA or impedance) and your waist; training, diet, and sleep dominate the actual outcome.

body_mass_index.consumer_value: BMI has genetic contributors but is a coarse signal. Use measured waist, body fat, and strength for a truer picture of body composition.

basal_metabolic_rate.consumer_value: Your genetic tendency for resting energy burn. Combine with measured weight, body composition, and activity; BMR genetics rarely explains weight on its own.

urate_levels.consumer_value: A higher urate tendency can mean higher gout risk and links to diet and metabolic health. If you've had gout or a high measured urate, this adds context; hydration, weight, and alcohol/purine intake matter.

creatinine_levels.consumer_value: A creatinine tendency, used to estimate kidney filtration. A measured creatinine or eGFR is the actionable value; the inherited signal is background.

calcium_levels.consumer_value: Genetic context for blood-calcium regulation. A measured calcium, alongside vitamin D and PTH when relevant, is what to interpret.

serum_phosphate_levels.consumer_value: Genetic context for blood phosphate. Interpret alongside a measured phosphate and kidney function.

serum_albumin_levels.consumer_value: Albumin is a general liver and nutrition protein marker. Use this tendency as context for a measured albumin result.

alkaline_phosphatase_levels.consumer_value: ALP is a liver- and bone-related enzyme. This is background for interpreting a measured ALP result, not a standalone signal.

bilirubin_levels.consumer_value: A bilirubin tendency, often reflecting Gilbert's-type variants. Mildly higher levels are usually benign; use this to interpret a blood result.

liver_iron_levels.consumer_value: Genetic context for how the liver handles iron. Pair with ferritin and iron studies; a measured panel matters far more than the tendency.

birth_weight.consumer_value: A largely historical trait with genetic contributors and limited day-to-day relevance now.

sleep_duration.consumer_value: Sleep need is meaningfully inherited (genetics may set up to ~45% of habitual duration). Compare this tendency with your tracked sleep; even natural short sleepers do better protecting a consistent, sufficient sleep window.

insomnia.consumer_value: A genetic lean toward insomnia; studies attribute roughly 38-59% of the variation to genetics. Read it as context, not a diagnosis; compare with your actual sleep patterns and see a clinician if sleepless nights persist.

morning_circadian_rythm_morning_person.consumer_value: Your inherited lean toward being a morning or evening person. Line it up with your real sleep timing and light exposure; consistent wake times and morning light shift your rhythm regardless of the tendency.

probability_of_snoring.consumer_value: A modest inherited tendency toward snoring (~18% heritable). Loud snoring with pauses can point to sleep apnea worth checking; compare with any sleep-tracking or partner reports.

cognitive_ability.consumer_value: A research-context signal for cognitive performance, shaped far more by education, environment, health, and sleep than by genes. Interest-only; never a measure of intelligence or potential.

mental_agility.consumer_value: A research signal for reasoning and processing speed. Age, sleep, stress, and practice move it far more than genetics; treat it as context, not a verdict.

neuroticisms.consumer_value: A small inherited component (~15%) of the tendency toward negative emotions. Current stress, sleep, support, and circumstances dominate; this is context, not a personality label or a mental-health prediction.

caffeine_and_anxiety.consumer_value: How your genes shape caffeine's anxiety-provoking effect via adenosine receptors. If caffeine makes you jittery, this adds context; try lower doses and earlier timing and watch your own response, resting HR, and sleep.

epigenetic_aging.consumer_value: Your genetic lean toward faster or slower biological aging (as read by DNA-methylation clocks). Aging is highly modifiable; pair this with a measured methylation age if you have one, plus sleep, training, and metabolic health, which move it most.

telomere_length.consumer_value: Telomere length is a partly inherited (~44%) cellular-aging marker that shortens with age and stress. A measured telomere test, plus sleep, exercise, and lower inflammation, tells you more than the tendency alone.

facial_aging.consumer_value: An inherited tendency for visible skin aging. Sun exposure, smoking, and skincare drive far more of the outcome than genetics; treat this as light context.

c_reactive_protein_levels.consumer_value: A genetic lean for CRP, a key inflammation marker. Your measured CRP (a simple blood test) is what to act on; a higher genetic tendency is a nudge to check it and address sleep, weight, and illness context.

white_blood_cell_count.consumer_value: A genetic tendency for overall white-blood-cell level. Your measured count is the number that counts; this adds context for interpreting it.

lymphocyte_count.consumer_value: An inherited tendency for lymphocyte levels, central immune cells. A measured blood count, read with infection and nutrition context, is the actionable value.

neutrophil_count.consumer_value: An inherited tendency for neutrophil levels, the front-line infection-fighting cells. A measured count is what to interpret; some ancestries run lower normal ranges.

monocyte_count.consumer_value: A genetic lean in monocyte levels, immune cells that fight infection. A measured count is the real signal; genetics is background.

eosinophil_count.consumer_value: A genetic tendency for eosinophil levels, immune cells tied to allergy and parasite response. Your measured blood count is what matters; genetics is background for interpreting it.

hla_b27_antigen.consumer_value: Whether you likely carry HLA-B27, a marker linked to certain autoimmune conditions (e.g. ankylosing spondylitis). A flag for clinical context, not a diagnosis; confirm with a proper HLA-B27 test if relevant.

ccr5delta32_and_susceptibility_to_hiv_in.consumer_value: Whether you carry the CCR5-delta32 deletion, which hampers HIV's entry into cells and can lower susceptibility. Inherited context only; it does not replace prevention or testing.

duffy_antigen_malaria_resistant.consumer_value: Duffy-antigen status, linked to resistance to Plasmodium vivax malaria and more common in African-ancestry populations. Ancestry-linked context, not a clinical instruction.

vitamin_d_levels.consumer_value: Vitamin D levels are partly inherited and strongly sun- and diet-driven. Measure serum 25-OH vitamin D before changing supplementation; a genetic lean toward low levels is a reason to test.

vitamin_b12_levels.consumer_value: B12 levels are notably inherited (~60% of the variation) alongside diet. A measured B12 is what to act on, especially on a low-animal-product diet; a genetic lean toward low levels is a reason to test.

vitamin_c_levels.consumer_value: A genetic tendency in vitamin C levels, alongside diet. A measured level plus fruit and veg intake is what matters; genetics is context.

vitamin_e_levels.consumer_value: A genetic lean in vitamin E levels. Deficiency is rare with a normal diet; a measured level is the real signal if there is concern.

levels_of_vitamin_a_beta_carotene.consumer_value: A genetic lean in circulating beta-carotene and vitamin A. A measured level plus colorful-vegetable intake is what to act on; genetics is background.

long_chain_omega_fatty_acids_levels.consumer_value: Your genetic tendency in omega-3/6 levels, fats you cannot fully make yourself. Pair with oily-fish intake or a measured omega-3 index rather than acting on the tendency alone.

lactose_intolerance.consumer_value: Whether you carry the variant that keeps the lactase enzyme active into adulthood (lactose tolerance). Compare with how dairy actually affects you; your symptoms are the real test.

histamine_intolerance.consumer_value: Your genetic capacity to break down dietary histamine (via the DAO and HNMT enzymes). If high-histamine foods trigger symptoms, this adds context; an elimination trial and clinical review confirm it.

alcohol_flush_reaction.consumer_value: Whether you carry ALDH2 variants that cause the alcohol 'flush' (facial redness, nausea) from slower alcohol breakdown. Flushing is a signal to drink less and is linked to higher risk from heavy drinking.

alcohol_dependence_after_prolonged_consu.consumer_value: A small inherited component (~5-10%) of alcohol-use tendency; environment and habit dominate. Light context, not a label; if drinking is a concern, behavior and support matter far more than genetics.

caffeine_and_sports_performance.consumer_value: How your CYP1A2 genetics shape caffeine's performance (ergogenic) effect. If you use caffeine before training, test dose and timing against your actual performance and how you feel.

caffeine_dependence_after_prolonged_cons.consumer_value: Genetic context for how much caffeine you tend to consume. Combine it with your own tolerance, sleep, and resting HR; the tendency is not a prescription.

antioxidant_capacity.consumer_value: Your genetic lean in antioxidant enzymes (glutathione peroxidase, catalase) that clear reactive oxygen species. Colorful-plant diet, sleep, and training influence this more; treat as context.

bitter_taste_perception.consumer_value: How strongly you taste bitter compounds (via TAS2R variants), which can nudge food and vegetable preferences. No health action; just self-knowledge.

preference_for_sweets.consumer_value: An inherited lean toward liking sweet foods. Useful self-awareness for managing intake; environment and habit are stronger drivers.

genetic_predisposition_to_peanut_allergy.consumer_value: An inherited lean in peanut-allergy risk. Genetics is only part of the picture; actual symptoms and clinical testing are decisive, so never self-diagnose from this.
